user_prompt:
Clean technical schematic of an end-to-end fNIRS Healthy-vs-GAD classification pipeline. IEEE/Nature paper aesthetic. Monochrome boxes-and-arrows with ONE accent color (teal or orange) used only for the final RESULT and XAI loop. NO photorealism, NO 3D rendering. White background. All tensor shapes in monospace. Wide horizontal banner aspect ratio.

Pipeline boxes left-to-right:
(1) PARTICIPANTS: two simple line-art human silhouettes labeled HC n=33 and GAD n=29; total n=62 subjects below.
(2) ACQUISITION: top-view headcap line-art icon showing 8 filled circles (sources S1-S8) and 8 open circles (detectors D1-D8) over a frontal area outline; arrow to next. Below: 'Go/No-Go task, 4 blocks of 32 s, sampling 10.17 Hz'.
(3) PREPROCESSING: vertical stack of three sub-boxes: 'Wavelet motion correction (IQR=1.5)', 'CBSI', 'Bandpass 0.01-0.5 Hz'. Output arrows labeled HbO/HbR/HbT.
(4) GRAPH CONSTRUCTION: small representative 8-12 node graph with edges, captioned: nodes = per-channel statistical features [23 x 6]; edges = |corr| >= 0.1 then edge_attr = (coherence, |corr|). Below: 'X in R^(23x6), edge_attr in R^(|E|x2)'.
(5) MODEL: two parallel branches stacked vertically:
  Top: 'SG - Spatial Graph (FlexibleGATNet)': GATv2 x 2 -> mean pool -> FC.
  Bottom: 'ST - Spatio-Temporal (WindowedSpatioTemporalGATNet)': window-stats -> shared GATv2 -> GRU -> additive attention -> FC.
(6) EVALUATION: three small labels stacked: '5-fold CV', '10-fold CV', 'LOSO 62 subjects'. Caption below: 'subject-level partitioning, no leakage'.
(7) RESULT: highlighted callout box in ACCENT color: 'ST x HbR x mt2 x LOSO -> F1 = 0.84'.
(8) EXPLAINABILITY (curved feedback arrow back from ST branch in ACCENT color): 'native attention' -> 'channel importance on 5x7 grid' -> 'Brodmann atlas (BA10/BA9/BA46)'.

Avoid: brain anatomy renderings, photographic textures, gradients in main flow, decorative icons. Use only sans-serif labels.

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.

early_stop_reason: Quality score 7.5 meets threshold 8.5 for journal
